what_it_does: Vitest test suite for buildCodexDoctorReport and trustStateFromHooksList, asserting report.ok, report.text substrings, version support floor, plugin hook and MCP state, hook trust, and log path behavior.
observations: Imports vitest describe/it/expect and two source modules with .js extensions.
buildCodexDoctorReport is exercised with full option objects including codexVersionOutput, featuresOutput, mcpListOutput, codexHome, sessionsDirExists, logPath, dbPath, and hookTrustState.
Expectations check both report.ok and substring matches in report.text.
A test verifies the report does not mention '/hooks' when hooks are already trusted.
A test verifies the report mentions '/hooks' and fails when hooks are untrusted.
A test verifies failure and an update hint when Codex version is below 0.130.0.
trustStateFromHooksList is tested against a nested hooks/list JSON shape with trustStatus values trusted, untrusted, and modified.
standouts: Tests both positive and negative wording of user-facing trust guidance.
Covers version support floor gating with an explicit minimum version string.
Maps Codex hooks/list trustStatus strings directly to expected parser output.
conventions: Uses substring containment assertions for report text.
Imports adjacent TypeScript source files with explicit .js extension.
Constructs input fixtures inline as object literals.
concerns: Large fixture objects are repeated almost verbatim across multiple tests.
sessionsDirExists and dbPath are passed but never asserted.
trustStateFromHooksList is only tested with a single episodic-memory hook per case.
No test for missing or mismatched pluginId in the hooks/list data.
notes: A behavioral test file that locks down the diagnostic report output and hook-trust parsing for the episodic-memory Codex integration.
questions: What exact logic does buildCodexDoctorReport use to compute report.ok and compose report.text?
How does the version comparator handle pre-release or non-semver version strings?
Why are sessionsDirExists and dbPath included in the report input if the tests do not assert them?
Is hookTrustState derived from trustStateFromHooksList in production, or are they independent inputs?
What should trustStateFromHooksList return when no episodic-memory hook is present?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it tests the Codex doctor report and hook trust parsing.
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to ensure the doctor command produces correct, actionable diagnostics for Codex CLI setup, especially around version compatibility, plugin hooks, MCP status, and hook trust.
contribution: Provides behavioral test coverage that preserves expected doctor report wording, success/failure transitions, and hook trust parsing for the episodic-memory integration.
summary_for_embedding: Vitest tests for a Codex doctor report builder and a hook-trust parser, covering version floor checks, plugin hook and MCP status, trust-state guidance, and log path reporting.
intention_for_embedding: To lock down the observable behavior of the diagnostic report and the trust-status extraction from Codex hooks/list output.
